Read:

* `.github/progress.md`

Find the last completed mission.

Open the following files from that mission:

* mission.md
* lab.md
* investigation.md
* solution.md
* notes.md
* submission.md
* diagram.md

Evaluate my submission as a Red Hat EX288 examiner.

Create a new file named `review.md` inside the same mission folder.

The review must contain:

# Mission Review

## Overall Score

Score: XX / 100

## Detailed Scores

| Category                        | Score |
| ------------------------------- | ----: |
| Mission Completion              |   /10 |
| OpenShift Understanding         |   /10 |
| Kubernetes Understanding        |   /10 |
| Troubleshooting                 |   /10 |
| CLI Usage                       |   /10 |
| YAML Quality                    |   /10 |
| Resource Relationships          |   /10 |
| Controllers & Reconciliation    |   /10 |
| API Server & etcd Understanding |   /10 |
| EX288 Readiness                 |   /10 |

## What I Did Well

List my strengths.

## Mistakes

List every mistake.

## Missing Knowledge

Explain concepts I don't fully understand.

## Better Solution

Show how an experienced OpenShift engineer would approach the problem.

## Internal Flow

Explain what happened inside OpenShift:

* API Server
* Controllers
* etcd
* Scheduler (if applicable)
* Kubelet (if applicable)

## Follow-up Questions

Ask 3–5 questions to verify my understanding.

## Harder Challenge

Give me one more difficult variation of this mission.

## Recommendation

Choose exactly one:

* Ready to continue
* Repeat this mission
* Review the notes before continuing

Do not be polite.

Do not inflate scores.

Grade me as if I were taking the EX288 exam.

The objective is deep understanding, not encouragement.
